reason: Medicare Advantage (MA) is a $300B+ private insurance market carved out of Medicare. Any 'improvement' bill targeting MA typically contains regulatory capture vectors—exemptions from rate-setting, benefit restrictions, or prior-auth rollbacks that benefit insurers at enrollee/CMS cost. Without summary text, title alone signals high audit priority given MA's history as regulatory arbitrage space.
vectors: Regulatory capture: MA insurers lobby aggressively for carve-outs from Medicare's cost controls
Fiscal bleed: MA overpayments to private plans vs. traditional Medicare baseline create recurring CMS loss vectors
Market-access exploit: bills framed as 'improvement' often restrict CMS's ability to enforce network adequacy or deny profit-maximizing benefit cuts
Entitlement erosion: coverage restrictions disguised as 'flexibility' or 'innovation' language
title: Medicare Advantage Improvement Act of 2026
